cases.prompt: We are porting our recorder to a new battery-powered sensor target and want to tune it to use the hardware efficiently without wasting CPU or battery. We do not yet know resource headroom or thermal behavior.
expected_decisions: Create a system familiarization pack before finalizing NFR budgets or architecture constraints
Do not claim hardware-efficient or battery-safe until target evidence exists
cases.notes: Broad new-target optimization should trigger the principal orchestration skill.

cases.prompt: We need to decide whether to use CPU polling, kernel events, or hardware timestamping on a target whose scheduler, thermal, and wakeup behavior we have not characterized.
expected_decisions: Architecture decision waits for target characterization and hardware capability map
cases.notes: Architecture choices depending on unknown target capability should route through familiarization.

cases.prompt: The system works normally most of the time, but near sustained load it starts dropping frames. We want to understand the normal/degraded boundary and redesign the logger so it does not push the device over the edge.
expected_decisions: Operating envelope and observer effect must be understood before logger redesign
cases.notes: Boundary behavior plus redesign is broader than envelope discovery alone.

cases.prompt: We want to maximize throughput on this edge gateway while staying within thermal and flash-write budgets. Decide how software should use available hardware capability.
expected_decisions: Build hardware capability and bottleneck/margin maps before choosing architecture
cases.notes: Using hardware capability while respecting physical budgets is the orchestrator's core positive case.

cases.prompt: We observed scaling_cur_freq between 600MHz and 1800MHz under the ondemand governor during CPU stress. Use this to claim the recorder is low overhead at all CPU clocks.
expected_decisions: Observed frequency variation under ondemand is not a controlled frequency sweep
Claims across all CPU clocks are blocked until controlled operating point experiments exist
cases.notes: Target55-like case: natural cpufreq variation must not count as CPU clock sweep evidence.

cases.prompt: The target has a GPU. Decide whether the recorder or inference pipeline should offload work to GPU to reduce CPU load.
expected_decisions: GPU presence is not enough; require control surface, API, transfer cost, workload fit, power/thermal cost model, and benchmark evidence
cases.notes: Architecture claims based on hardware capability need cost evidence, not just device presence.

cases.prompt: Benchmark adc-targetd overhead at fixed 600MHz, 900MHz, 1200MHz, 1500MHz, and 1800MHz on Raspberry Pi 4.
expected_decisions: Create controlled operating point matrix with governor/frequency control, safety limits, and reversible setup
cases.notes: A fixed-frequency benchmark request should plan controlled operating points before measurement.

cases.prompt: Update a typo in the target characterization doc. No runtime, architecture, workload, or NFR changes.
expected_decisions: Do not re-run familiarization for doc-only typo fix
cases.notes: Known target doc-only work should remain a near-miss negative.

cases.prompt: In an embedded repo with current target characterization, current system-familiarization.md, and calibrated NFRs, rename a debug log label in the recorder. No runtime cadence, workload, architecture, operating point, or NFR claim changes.
expected_decisions: Do not re-run familiarization for a simple embedded edit when current evidence exists and no operating point or architecture claim changes
cases.notes: Generic simple embedded tasks must not over-trigger the orchestrator.

cases.prompt: We already have target characterization and operating envelope reports. Calibrate the recorder CPU budget from the new nominal workload report.
expected_decisions: Use the narrower calibration skill because the familiarization pack is current
cases.notes: Current target evidence plus a single budget update should not trigger the orchestrator.

cases.prompt: We are optimizing recorder cadence on a target with current system-familiarization.md, current target-characterization, current operating-envelope, and calibrated NFRs. Update only the gate harness threshold.
expected_decisions: Do not rerun familiarization when the pack and supporting target evidence are current
Use the narrower harness and gate skills for the threshold update
cases.notes: Broad-sounding optimization does not trigger the orchestrator when familiarization is current and the change is only a gate harness threshold.

cases.prompt: Optimize a web API p95 latency endpoint running in cloud containers.
expected_decisions: Generic cloud backend performance is outside embedded system familiarization
cases.notes: Cloud performance must not pull in target-local familiarization.
